blocks.description: Secrets hardcoded in source code end up in Git history forever. Even if you delete them later, anyone who clones the repo can find them.
blocks.code:
# NEVER DO THIS -- these secrets are now in your Git history forever
DATABASE_URL = "postgresql://admin:supersecret123@db.example.com:5432/myapp"
SECRET_KEY = "my-super-secret-flask-key"
STRIPE_API_KEY = "sk_live_51ABC123DEF456"
SENDGRID_API_KEY = "SG.abc123def456.xyz789"
AWS_ACCESS_KEY = "AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE"
AWS_SECRET_KEY = "wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY"

blocks.description: Load secrets from environment variables. Use python-dotenv for local development and your platform's secret manager in production.
blocks.code:
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv()  # Loads .env file in development

class Config:
    DATABASE_URL = os.environ["DATABASE_URL"]
    SECRET_KEY = os.environ["SECRET_KEY"]
    STRIPE_API_KEY = os.environ["STRIPE_API_KEY"]
    SENDGRID_API_KEY = os.environ["SENDGRID_API_KEY"]

# --- .env (never committed to Git) ---
# DATABASE_URL=postgresql://admin:supersecret123@localhost:5432/myapp
# SECRET_KEY=generate-a-random-string-here
# STRIPE_API_KEY=sk_test_...
# SENDGRID_API_KEY=SG...

# --- .gitignore (MUST include .env) ---
# .env
# .env.local
# .env.production
